 {"id":"ntm-161","title":"Implement ntm mail inbox command","description":"# Implement ntm mail inbox Command

## Purpose

Display an AGGREGATE inbox view showing messages across ALL agents in the project, not just NTM's coordinator messages.

## IMPORTANT: Aggregate View

This is not just NTM's inbox. It shows:
1. Messages TO any agent in the project (GreenCastle, BlueLake, etc.)
2. Messages FROM any agent in the project
3. Filtered by session agents if desired

This gives the user visibility into all agent-to-agent communication.

## Command Syntax

```bash
# Show all messages in project (across all agents)
ntm mail inbox myproject

# Filter to session's agents only
ntm mail inbox myproject --session-agents

# Filter to specific agent
ntm mail inbox myproject --agent GreenCastle

# Urgent only
ntm mail inbox myproject --urgent

# JSON for scripts
ntm mail inbox myproject --json
```

## Output Format

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Project Inbox: myproject (5 agents)                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● #42 [URGENT] API Review Needed                            │
│   GreenCastle → BlueLake                                    │
│   2 minutes ago │ Thread: FEAT-123 │ ⚠️ Ack required         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ○ #41 Tests passing, ready for merge                        │
│   RedStone → GreenCastle, BlueLake                          │
│   15 minutes ago │ Thread: FEAT-123                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ○ #40 Starting work on src/api/**                           │
│   ntm_myproject → all                                       │
│   30 minutes ago │ (coordinator announcement)               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Showing messages involving: GreenCastle, BlueLake, RedStone, ntm_myproject
```

## Implementation

1. Discover all agents in project via Agent Mail
2. Fetch inbox for EACH agent
3. Deduplicate (same message may appear in multiple inboxes)
4. Sort by timestamp
5. Display with sender → recipient format

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shows messages across ALL project agents
- [ ] Deduplication (message shown once even if multiple recipients)
- [ ] Filter by --session-agents, --agent, --urgent
- [ ] Clear indication of sender → recipient
- [ ] Coordinator messages distinguished from agent messages
- [ ] --json output for scripts","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:48.350455-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:01:19.314483-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-161","depends_on_id":"ntm-yxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.9288-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-1ts","title":"Implement ntm lock/unlock file reservation commands","description":"# Implement ntm lock/unlock File Reservation Commands

## Purpose

Expose Agent Mail's file reservation system as NTM commands, allowing agents to declare intent before editing files and avoid conflicts.

## Command Syntax

```bash
# Reserve files for editing
ntm lock myproject "src/api/**" --reason "Implementing user endpoints"

# Reserve multiple patterns
ntm lock myproject "src/api/**" "tests/api/**"

# With TTL (default 1 hour)
ntm lock myproject "config/*.json" --ttl 2h

# Shared (read) vs exclusive (write) lock
ntm lock myproject "docs/**" --shared

# Release specific locks
ntm unlock myproject "src/api/**"

# Release all locks for session
ntm unlock myproject --all

# Show current locks
ntm locks myproject
ntm locks myproject --all-agents  # Include other sessions
```

## Lock Display Format

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ File Reservations: myproject                                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 🔒 src/api/**                                               │
│   Agent: ntm_myproject │ Exclusive │ Expires in 45m        │
│   Reason: Implementing user endpoints                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 🔒 tests/api/**                                             │
│   Agent: ntm_myproject │ Exclusive │ Expires in 45m        │
│   Reason: Implementing user endpoints                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 📖 docs/** (shared)                                         │
│   Agent: GreenCastle │ Shared │ Expires in 1h30m           │
│   Reason: Updating API documentation                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Conflict Detection

When attempting to lock:
```
⚠️ Conflict detected:
  Pattern: src/api/**
  Held by: GreenCastle (cc_1)
  Reason: Refactoring auth module
  Expires in: 25 minutes

Options:
  1. Wait for expiry
  2. Request release: ntm mail send myproject --to cc_1 "Need src/api/**"
  3. Force lock (dangerous): ntm lock myproject "src/api/**" --force
```

## Integration with spawn/add

When spawning agents:
```bash
# Check for conflicts before spawning
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --check-locks

# Auto-reserve working directory
ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --reserve-cwd
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lock patterns with glob support
- [ ] TTL specification (default 1h, max 24h)
- [ ] Exclusive vs shared locks
- [ ] Conflict detection with helpful messages
- [ ] ntm locks command shows current state
- [ ] --all-agents shows all project reservations
- [ ] Integration hooks for spawn command","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCastle","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:42.508839-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:39:33.733237-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T04:39:33.733237-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-1ts","depends_on_id":"ntm-yxa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:56.109325-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-20n","title":"Implement --robot-assign for work distribution recommendations","description":"AI-assisted work assignment suggestions based on current state.

## Rationale
With N agents and M ready beads, optimal assignment is non-trivial. This flag analyzes current state and recommends which agent should work on what, considering:
- Agent types and their strengths
- Current workload balance  
- Bead dependencies and priorities
- Recent activity patterns

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-assign myproject
ntm --robot-assign myproject --beads=ntm-abc,ntm-def  # specific beads
ntm --robot-assign myproject --strategy=balanced      # or: speed, quality
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "strategy": "balanced",
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "agent": "0.1",
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "assign_bead": "ntm-1rz",
      "confidence": 0.85,
      "reasoning": "Profiler task matches claude's code analysis strengths"
    },
    {
      "agent": "0.2", 
      "agent_type": "codex",
      "assign_bead": "ntm-7mg",
      "confidence": 0.72,
      "reasoning": "UI/styling task, codex handles CSS well"
    }
  ],
  "blocked_beads": [
    {"id": "ntm-xyz", "blocked_by": ["ntm-abc"]}
  ],
  "idle_agents": ["0.3"],
  "unassignable_beads": [
    {"id": "ntm-qqq", "reason": "Requires human input"}
  ]
}
```

## Assignment Strategies
- balanced: distribute work evenly across agents
- speed: assign to fastest/most active agents  
- quality: match task types to agent strengths
- dependency: prioritize unblocking work

## Integration with Beads
Requires parsing bd ready and bd show output to understand task graph. Consider caching bead state to avoid repeated queries.

## Future Enhancement
Could integrate with agent-mail to check agent's stated preferences or current focus.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedCat","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:51:50.71632-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:21:29.033695-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-gcv","title":"Implement notification system with desktop and webhook support","description":"Create a notification system that can alert users via multiple channels.

## What This Task Does

Build infrastructure to send notifications when events occur:
- Desktop notifications (native OS)
- Webhook POST (Slack, Discord, custom)
- Shell command execution
- Log file appending

## Notification Events

- agent.error - Agent hit error state
- agent.crashed - Agent process exited
- agent.restarted - Agent was auto-restarted
- agent.idle - Agent waiting for input
- session.created - New session spawned
- session.killed - Session terminated
- health.degraded - Overall health dropped

## Configuration

[notifications]
enabled = true
events = ["agent.error", "agent.crashed", "agent.restarted"]

[notifications.desktop]
enabled = true

[notifications.webhook]
url = "https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX/YYY/ZZZ"
# Payload template (Go template syntax)
template = '''
{"text": "NTM: {{.Event}} - {{.Message}}"}
'''

[notifications.shell]
command = "~/.config/ntm/notify.sh"
pass_json = true

[notifications.log]
path = "~/.local/share/ntm/notifications.jsonl"

## Implementation

Create internal/notify package:

type Event struct {
    Type      string
    Session   string
    Agent     string
    Message   string
    Timestamp time.Time
    Data      map[string]interface{}
}

type Notifier interface {
    Notify(event Event) error
}

Implementations:
- DesktopNotifier: osascript (macOS), notify-send (Linux)
- WebhookNotifier: HTTP POST with template rendering
- ShellNotifier: exec.Command with JSON stdin
- LogNotifier: Append to JSONL file

## Desktop Notifications

macOS:
osascript -e 'display notification "message" with title "NTM"'

Linux (notify-send):
notify-send "NTM" "message"

Windows/WSL:
powershell.exe -Command "[Windows.UI.Notifications...]"

## ntm notify command

Manual notification for testing/scripting:

$ ntm notify "Build complete" --session myproject
$ ntm notify --event agent.error --message "Rate limit hit"

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Desktop notifications work on macOS and Linux
- [ ] Webhook POST sends correctly formatted payload
- [ ] Shell command receives event as JSON
- [ ] Events can be filtered in config
- [ ] ntm notify command for manual triggers
- [ ] Errors in notification don't break NTM

 {"id":"ntm-gk5","title":"Integrate agent-mail into robot snapshot","description":"Include agent-mail status in unified snapshot.

## Rationale
Agent-mail is how agents coordinate. The snapshot needs to include mail status so orchestrator knows about pending communications.

## Integration Points
1. Check if agent-mail project exists for current working dir
2. Query agent-mail for registered agents
3. Map tmux panes to agent-mail identities
4. Include unread counts and pending ack counts

## Snapshot Extension
```json
{
  "...": "existing fields",
  "agent_mail": {
    "project": "/path/to/project",
    "agents": {
      "BlueLake": {"pane": "0.1", "unread": 2, "pending_ack": 1},
      "GreenCastle": {"pane": "0.2", "unread": 0, "pending_ack": 0}
    },
    "total_unread": 2,
    "threads_active": 3
  }
}
```

## Challenges
- Agent names in mail != pane identifiers (need mapping)
- Agent-mail may not be installed/configured
- Multiple projects may be active

## Graceful Degradation
If agent-mail not available, include:
```json
{"agent_mail": {"available": false, "reason": "not configured"}}

 {"id":"ntm-gon","title":"EPIC: Clipboard Integration","description":"Seamless copy/paste between agents and system clipboard.

## Problem Statement

Copying content from agents requires manual tmux operations:
1. Enter tmux copy mode (prefix + [)
2. Navigate to content start
3. Start selection
4. Navigate to content end
5. Copy to tmux buffer
6. Exit copy mode
7. Paste to system clipboard (separate step)

This friction discourages quick capture of agent outputs, code snippets,
and error messages. Users often retype or screenshot instead.

## Goals

1. One-command copy from any pane to system clipboard
2. Support filtering (last N lines, code blocks only, regex match)
3. Pipe clipboard contents to agents easily
4. Cross-platform clipboard support (macOS, Linux, WSL)

## Why This Matters

Fast copy/paste enables:
- Quick extraction of generated code
- Easy sharing of agent outputs
- Rapid iteration on prompts (copy response, modify, resend)
- Documentation capture
- Error reporting workflows

The goal is zero-friction capture: see something useful, capture it instantly.

## Success Criteria

- ntm copy myproject:1 copies pane 1 to clipboard
- ntm copy myproject --last 50 copies last 50 lines
- ntm copy myproject --pattern 'ERROR.*' copies matching lines
- ntm copy myproject:1 --code extracts code blocks only
- pbpaste | ntm send myproject --cc sends clipboard to agent
- Works on macOS (pbcopy), Linux (xclip/xsel), and WSL (clip.exe)

## Technical Approach

Platform Detection:
- macOS: pbcopy/pbpaste
- Linux X11: xclip -selection clipboard
- Linux Wayland: wl-copy/wl-paste
- WSL: clip.exe / powershell.exe Get-Clipboard
- Fallback: tmux's built-in clipboard with warning

Copy Pipeline:
1. Capture pane content (existing CapturePaneOutput)
2. Apply filters (line limit, regex, code extraction)
3. Pipe to platform clipboard command

Code Block Extraction:
- Parse markdown code fences from agent output
- Extract language hint if present
- Option to include/exclude fence markers
- Future: integrate with ntm-jar (Output Intelligence)

## Example Workflows

Capture last command output:
$ ntm copy myproject:1 --last 100

Capture only code blocks from agent response:
$ ntm copy myproject:1 --code
# Copies: function authenticate(token) { ... }

Send clipboard as prompt context:
$ pbpaste | ntm send myproject --cc 'Review this code:'

## Interaction with Other Epics

- Output Intelligence (ntm-jar): Advanced code extraction
- JSON Output: Clipboard content in JSON mode
- Remote Sessions: Clipboard is local, content comes from remote

## Design Decisions

Why shell out to clipboard commands?
- Cross-platform compatibility
- Leverages user's existing clipboard setup
- No CGo or native dependencies
- Works with clipboard managers

Why not integrate directly with tmux clipboard?
- Tmux buffer is separate from system clipboard
- Extra step to sync to system clipboard
- Direct approach is more user-friendly

## Future Possibilities

- ntm paste: Insert clipboard content into active pane
- Clipboard history integration
- Smart content detection (JSON, URL, file path)
- IDE integration (copy to editor's clipboard)
